create_module_page.title: Create Module
create_module_page.create_form.tooltip: The module title is visible in your workspace and will be displayed publicly when published; it can be changed in Project Settings.
create_module_page.create_form.module_id: The module identifier appears in your workspace url and can be used to reference your module in project code. <1>It cannot be changed.</1>
create_module_page.create_form.error.inv: A module ID must be at least 4 characters long and cannot contain any spaces, uppercase letters, or special characters.
module_settings_page.compatibility_setti: Module compatibility determines which types of projects can use this module. Changing this setting also changes how your module's assets are loaded into an experience. Read more in <documentationLink>the documentation</documentationLink>.
Please select at least one project type.
module_settings_page.publish_settings.he: Publish your module so that it can appear on your public profile page and others can import it into their projects. <linkDocs>Learn more about module publishing and distribution</linkDocs>.
module_settings_page.publish_settings.mi: Module needs to have a deployed version before it can be published.
module_settings_page.publish_settings.ac: To publish this module you need to activate your Public Profile. You must be a Workspace Owner or Admin to make changes to the Public Profile page.
module_settings_page.publish_settings.ag: By publishing, you agree to the <linkTos>Terms of Service</linkTos> and <linkGuidelines>Content Guidelines</linkGuidelines>, and acknowledge the <linkPrivacy>Privacy Policy.</linkPrivacy>
module_settings_page.publish_settings.ar: Archive your module to hide it from on your public profile and remove it from the import modal.
An archived module will still be available on any public project it was previously imported to. You can cannot create new deployments for archived modules.
module_settings_page.publish_settings.co: Allow everyone to view and fork your module's code.
module_settings_page.publish_settings.im: Images and GIFs will appear in the order that you upload.
Unable to save media. Please reupload.
module_settings_page.publish_settings.in: Module Published! You can view the module at <linkTo>{{modulePublicUrl}}</linkTo>
module_settings_page.publish_settings.ov: Describe your module. Help users learn about what your module provides, how it works, and key use cases. Showcase your module with media, images, and links to relevant projects or other key support resources.
module_settings_page.publish_settings.pu: In order for your code to be public and/or forkable, you need a compatible license. Add your preferred public license directly to your module code and deploy.

A module with the {{licenseType}} license can be imported and forked. Developers can access the configuration settings and choose to fork the code to have full access to edit the code to their needs. The module will appear on your Public Profile and may appear in public discovery surfaces. Your module overview, README, code and assets will be publicly available.
module_settings_page.publish_settings.ta: Start typing tags. Separate using commas, hit backspace to delete. Maximum of 6 tags, up to 30 characters each.
module_settings_page.publish_settings.vi: Paste a URL to a YouTube or Vimeo video to embed it on your public module.
Please enter a valid YouTube or Vimeo URL
